story: My book club introduced me to Adam Ezra Group last spring. Every chorus feels like a hug. I've been collecting songs about friendship since.
My older brother had this on heavy rotation when I visited him at college. The story-song format ruined three-minute pop for me forever.
My college boyfriend was a Zeppelin completist. He played this on a rainy Sunday and I finally understood what slow blues could do.
My mother sang this to me when I had trouble sleeping. I sang it to my own children. It will outlive me.
I came to this song late. Hearing Cohen sing it himself was a different thing entirely from the covers.
